LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BIOMARIN PHARMACEUTICAL INC.'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF UNLIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. STOCK PURCHASE AGREEMENT DOESN'T PROVIDE FOR THE RELIEF REQUESTED. NO SET TIME LIMIT PROVIDED FOR OTHER THAN 90-DAY DEADLINE IN SECTION 6.1A(2). JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-09-2009 Off Calendar

Plaintiff'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Biomarin Pharmaceutical Inc.'s Motion for Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: John A. Reding, Esq. and Edward Han, Esq. of Paul Hastings (415) 856-7085, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Lloyd Winawer, Esq. and Susanne N. Geraghty, Esq. of Goodwing Procter (415) 733-6000, appearing for Defendant, La Jolla Pharmaceutical Company, and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied. Plaintiff unlikely to prevail on the merits. Stock purchase agreement doesn't provide for the relief requested. No set time limit provided for other than 90-day deadline in section 6.1A(2). (302/CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
